LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. DEFENDANT MIGUEL BUSTOS' MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S RELEASE OF AUGUST 25, 2006, THAT INCLUDES ALL KNOWN AND UNKNOWN CLAIMS, LIMITS THE CONDUCT AT ISSUE TO EVENTS SUBSEQUENT TO THAT DATE. THE ONLY ALLEGEDLY PROTECTED CONDUCT IS THE FILING OF THE ELLIS ACT NOTICE TO WITHDRAW THE PROPERTY FROM THE RENTAL MARKET. THE FILING OF THE ELLIS ACT NOTICE IS NOT A FORM OF PETITIONING THE GOVERNMENT BECAUSE THE ELLIS ACT IS A SELF-EXECUTING STATUTE AND REQUIRES NO RESPONSE FROM THE GOVERNMENT. THE ELLIS ACT NOTICE IS NOT PROTECTED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE. IT IS TOO REMOTE IN TIME FOR IT TO BE FILED IN CONTEMPLATION OF LITIGATION AND TO EXTENT IT MAY BE CONSIDERED PRIVILEGED, THE FILING HAS A DUAL PURPOSE WITHIN THE MEANING OF STACY & WITBECK V CCSF(1996)47 CAL. APP.4TH 1, 7-8. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE OF SEP-26-2007 CONTINUED TO NOV-14-2007 AT 10:00 AM IN DEPT. 212. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

HEARING ON APPLICANT/PLAINTIFF JESUS BUSTOS' WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS. NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE APPLICANT'S INCOME IS ENOUGH TO PAY THE COMMON NECCESARIES OF LIFE AND COURT FEES AND COSTS. JESUS BUSTOS HAS TEN DAYS TO PAY THE FILING FEES OR THE FILINGS WILL BE STRICKEN. MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
SHANNON H. SAGUM, ESQ. OF BLEDSOE LLP, (415) 981-5411, FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY MIGUEL BUSTOS
JESSICA ALEXANDRA, ESQ. AND AARON MORRISON, ESQ.(LAW OFFICES OF BEHREND, MORRISON & ALEXANDRA, (415) 956-0549) FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY, JESUS BUSTOS

TENTATIVE RULING WAS A HEARING IS REQUIRED.

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. DEFENDANT MIGUEL BUSTOS' Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs' Complaint IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S RELEASE OF AUGUST 25, 2006, THAT INCLUDES ALL KNOWN AND UNKNOWN CLAIMS, LIMITS THE CONDUCT AT ISSUE TO EVENTS SUBSEQUENT TO THAT DATE. THE ONLY ALLEGEDLY PROTECTED CONDUCT IS THE FILING OF THE ELLIS ACT NOTICE TO WITHDRAW THE PROPERTY FROM THE RENTAL MARKET. THE FILING OF THE ELLIS ACT NOTICE IS NOT A FORM OF PETITIONING THE GOVERNMENT BECAUSE THE ELLIS ACT IS A SELF-EXECUTING STATUTE AND REQUIRES NO RESPONSE FROM THE GOVERNMENT. THE ELLIS ACT NOTICE IS NOT PROTECTED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE. IT IS TOO REMOTE IN TIME FOR IT TO BE FILED IN CONTEMPLATION OF LITIGATION AND TO EXTENT IT MAY BE CONSIDERED PRIVILEGED, THE FILING HAS A DUAL PURPOSE WITHIN THE MEANING OF STACY & WITBECK V CCSF(1996)47 CAL. APP.4TH 1, 7-8. (302/PJM/AP) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Ntc Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Pltfs Complaint
